ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 304, NO APPEARANCES, NOT CONTESTED, AND THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED UNDER C.C.P. 36(A). NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE TRIAL DATE IS JULY 21, 2025, AT 11:15 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 206. LAST DAY PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 36(F) IS JULY 30, 2025. THE LAST DAY FOR HEARING SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ADJUDICATION MOTIONS IS JULY 15, 2025. A FURTHER CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR JUNE 3, 2025, AT 4:00 P.M. A MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE IS SET FOR THURSDAY, JULY 17, 2025, AT 9:30 A.M.IN DEPARTMENT 503. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN. CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA. NOT REPORTED. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE - TRIAL COURTS
Off Calendar May-30-2025 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Apr-01-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, not contested, and the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion for Order Granting Preference in Setting Case for Trial, and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. 36(a). No Opposition filed.

1. The trial date is July 21, 2025, at 11:15 a.m. in Department 206.
Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is July 30, 2025.
2. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is July 15, 2025.
3. Time to respond to written discovery not yet served is shortened to 20 days.
4. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service.
5. The fact discovery cut-off date is July 3, 2025.
6. The expert discovery cut-off date is July 18, 2025.
7. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than May 1, 2025.
8. No later than April 15, 2025:
a. Plaintiff must provide a statement to each Defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting Plaintiff's claims as to each Defendant.
b. Defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of facts supporting Defendant's affirmative defenses.
c. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary judgment motion.
9. A further case management conference is set for June 3, 2025, at 4:00 p.m.
10. Plaintiffs will send a settlement demand to Defendants no later than June 9, 2025. Response to the settlement demand is due June 23, 2025. The parties shall separately submit confidential settlement demands/offers. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Department503@sftc.org via electronic mail on June 16, 2025.
11. A mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, July 17, 2025, at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is excused for good cause. A request to excuse a personal appearance must be submitted to Department503@sftc.org two weeks before the mandatory settlement conference.

Order signed. (See order filed this date for the court's complete ruling.)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Not Reported. Bailiff: Not Present. (304/EPS)
